The genus Elytranthe is thought to be closely related to the genus Parsonsia, also within the family Apocynaceae. intro: Elytranthe tetragona, also known as Chinese foxglove or Chinese foxglove creeper, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
